11	//! Rust MIR to `dialect-mir` translator and compilation pipeline for cuda-oxide.
12	//!
13	//! This crate translates Rust's Mid-level Intermediate Representation (MIR)
14	//! into [`dialect-mir`][dialect_mir] — a pliron dialect (MLIR-like) that
15	//! preserves Rust semantics — then drives the rest of the compilation pipeline
16	//! down to PTX.
17	//!
18	//! # Architecture
19	//!
20	//! ```text
21	//! ┌─────────────────────── mir-importer ──────────────────────────────────┐
22	//! │                                                                       │
23	//! │  ┌──────────────┐   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
24	//! │  │  translator  │──▶│                  pipeline                   │   │
25	//! │  │              │   │                                             │   │
26	//! │  │     MIR      │   │  dialect-mir (alloca)                       │   │
27	//! │  │      ──▶     │   │    ──▶ mem2reg                              │   │
28	//! │  │  dialect-mir │   │    ──▶ dialect-mir (SSA)                    │   │
29	//! │  │   (alloca)   │   │    ──▶ annotated loop unroll                │   │
30	//! │  │              │   │    ──▶ LLVM dialect  (via mir-lower)        │   │
31	//! │  │              │   │    ──▶ LLVM IR ──▶ PTX  (via llc)           │   │
32	//! │  └──────────────┘   └─────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
33	//! │                                                                       │
34	//! └───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
35	//! ```
36	//!
37	//! # Key Modules
38	//!
39	//! | Module         | Purpose                                                     |
40	//! |----------------|-------------------------------------------------------------|
41	//! | [`translator`] | MIR → `dialect-mir` (alloca + load/store)                   |
42	//! | [`pipeline`]   | `mem2reg`, unroll, lower, export LLVM IR, run llc           |
43	//! | [`error`]      | Error types integrated with pliron's error system           |
44	//!
45	//! Note: Function collection is handled by `rustc-codegen-cuda/src/collector.rs`
46	//! which uses rustc internals for efficient traversal.
47	//!
48	//! # Example
49	//!
50	//! ```rust,ignore
51	//! use pliron::context::Context;
52	//! use rustc_public::mir::mono::Instance;
53	//!
54	//! // Inside rustc callback:
55	//! let body = instance.body().unwrap();
56	//! let mut ctx = Context::new();
57	//!
58	//! let module_op = mir_importer::translator::translate_function(
59	//!     &mut ctx, &body, &instance, /* is_kernel */ true
60	//! )?;
61	//! ```
62	//!
63	//! # Alloca + load/store model
64	//!
65	//! Every non-ZST MIR local is materialised as a single `mir.alloca` emitted
66	//! at the top of the function's entry block. Defs lower to `mir.store`, uses
67	//! lower to `mir.load`. Cross-block data flow happens through the slots, so
68	//! blocks (other than the entry) take no arguments. Pliron's `mem2reg` pass
69	//! promotes the slots back into SSA before the `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect
70	//! lowering runs.

9	//! # `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect Lowering
10	//!
11	//! This crate implements the lowering pass that converts
12	//! [`dialect-mir`][dialect_mir] operations into LLVM dialect operations
13	//! (provided by `pliron-llvm`, re-exported via [`llvm_export`]), with
14	//! GPU-specific operations lowered to inline PTX assembly or NVVM
15	//! intrinsic calls.
16	//!
17	//! ## Overview
18	//!
19	//! `mir-lower` bridges cuda-oxide's Rust-semantic dialect (`dialect-mir`)
20	//! to the LLVM dialect. After lowering, ordinary PTX builds go directly to
21	//! `llvm-export`. NVVM builds first pass through `nvvm-transforms`, which
22	//! adjusts the LLVM module for the selected libNVVM dialect.
23	//!
24	//! ## Compilation Pipeline Position
25	//!
26	//! ```text
27	//! Rust Source Code
28	//!        │
29	//!        ▼
30	//! ┌──────────────┐
31	//! │   rustc      │  (extracts Stable MIR)
32	//! └──────┬───────┘
33	//!        │
34	//!        ▼
35	//! ┌──────────────┐
36	//! │ mir-importer │  (Stable MIR → dialect-mir, mem2reg, annotated unroll)
37	//! └──────┬───────┘
38	//!        │
39	//!        ▼
40	//! ┌──────────────┐
41	//! │  mir-lower   │  ◄── THIS CRATE (dialect-mir → LLVM dialect)
42	//! └──────┬───────┘
43	//!        │
44	//!        ▼
45	//! ┌──────────────┐
46	//! │ llvm-export  │  (exports to LLVM IR)
47	//! └──────┬───────┘
48	//!        │
49	//!        ▼
50	//! ┌──────────────┐
51	//! │     llc      │  (LLVM IR → PTX)
52	//! └──────────────┘
53	//! ```
54	//!
55	//! ## Architecture
56	//!
57	//! The crate uses pliron's `DialectConversion` framework for the lowering
58	//! pass. The framework handles IR walking, def-before-use ordering, type
59	//! conversion, and block argument patching automatically. Each
60	//! `dialect-mir` / `dialect-nvvm` op declares its own conversion via the
61	//! `MirToLlvmConversion` op interface.
62	//!
63	//! ### Core Modules
64	//!
65	//! - **[`conversion_interface`]**: The `MirToLlvmConversion` op interface
66	//!   trait. Each `dialect-mir` / `dialect-nvvm` op implements this to
67	//!   declare how it lowers to the LLVM dialect.
68	//!
69	//! - **[`context`]**: CUDA-specific state maps (`SharedGlobalsMap`,
70	//!   `DynamicSmemAlignmentMap`) used during conversion.
71	//!
72	//! - **[`helpers`]**: Utility functions for creating LLVM dialect
73	//!   constants, declaring intrinsics, and navigating the IR hierarchy.
74	//!
75	//! ### Conversion Modules ([`convert`])
76	//!
77	//! - **[`convert::types`]**: Type conversion from `dialect-mir` types to
78	//!   LLVM dialect types.
79	//!
80	//! - **[`convert::ops`]**: Operation converters organized by semantic category:
81	//!   - `arithmetic` - Binary/unary math operations
82	//!   - `memory` - Load, store, alloca, pointer arithmetic
83	//!   - `control_flow` - Branch, return, assert
84	//!   - `constants` - Integer and float constants
85	//!   - `cast` - Type conversions (int↔float, widening, narrowing)
86	//!   - `aggregate` - Struct/tuple/enum operations
87	//!   - `call` - Function calls
88	//!
89	//! - **[`convert::intrinsics`]**: GPU intrinsic converters:
90	//!   - `basic` - Thread/block IDs, barrier
91	//!   - `warp` - Shuffle, vote operations
92	//!   - `mbarrier` - Asynchronous barriers (Hopper+)
93	//!   - `tma` - Tensor Memory Accelerator (Hopper+)
94	//!   - `wgmma` - Warpgroup Matrix Multiply-Accumulate (Hopper)
95	//!   - `tcgen05` - 5th-gen Tensor Core (Blackwell)
96	//!   - `stmatrix` - Shared memory matrix store
97	//!
98	//! ## Usage
99	//!
100	//! ```ignore
101	//! use mir_lower::lower_mir_to_llvm;
102	//! use pliron::context::Context;
103	//!
104	//! let mut ctx = Context::new();
105	//! // ... register dialects, translate MIR into dialect-mir ...
106	//!
107	//! lower_mir_to_llvm(&mut ctx, module_op)?;
108	//!
109	//! // module_op now contains LLVM dialect operations
110	//! ```
111	//!
112	//! ## GPU Intrinsic Lowering Strategy
113	//!
114	//! GPU intrinsics are lowered using two strategies:
115	//!
116	//! 1. **LLVM Intrinsic Calls**: For operations with direct NVVM intrinsic
117	//!    equivalents (e.g., `llvm_nvvm_read_ptx_sreg_tid_x` for thread ID).
118	//!
119	//! 2. **Inline PTX Assembly**: For complex operations without direct intrinsics,
120	//!    or where inline PTX provides better control (e.g., tcgen05, wgmma MMA).
121	//!    Uses `llvm.inlineasm` with the `convergent` attribute for warp-synchronous
122	//!    semantics.
123	
124	#![warn(missing_docs)]
125	
126	pub mod context;
127	pub mod conversion_interface;
128	pub mod convert;
129	pub mod helpers;
130	pub mod lowering;
131	pub mod type_conversion_interface;

133	use rustc_hash::FxHashMap;
134	
135	use pliron::{
136	    builtin::types::{IntegerType, Signedness},
137	    context::{Context, Ptr},
138	    irbuild::dialect_conversion::{
139	        DialectConversion, DialectConversionRewriter, OperandsInfo, apply_dialect_conversion,
140	    },
141	    location::Located,
142	    op::{Op, op_cast},
143	    operation::Operation,
144	    result::Result,
145	    r#type::{TypeHandle, Typed, type_impls},
146	};
147	
148	use context::{DeviceGlobalsMap, DynamicSmemAlignmentMap, SharedGlobalsMap};
149	use conversion_interface::MirToLlvmConversion as MirToLlvmConversionInterface;
150	use convert::types::convert_type;
151	use type_conversion_interface::MirConvertibleType;
152	
153	// ============================================================================
154	// DialectConversion driver
155	// ============================================================================
156	
157	/// `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect conversion driver.
158	///
159	/// Implements pliron's `DialectConversion` trait. The `rewrite` method uses
160	/// `op_cast`-based dispatch via the `MirToLlvmConversion` op interface,
161	/// so each `dialect-mir` / `dialect-nvvm` op declares its own lowering.
162	///
163	/// Holds CUDA-specific state that certain ops need during conversion:
164	/// shared-memory global deduplication and dynamic shared-memory alignment.
165	pub struct MirToLlvmConversionDriver {
166	    /// Shared memory global deduplication across all functions.
167	    pub shared_globals: SharedGlobalsMap,
168	    /// Device global deduplication across all functions.
169	    pub device_globals: DeviceGlobalsMap,
170	    /// Per-kernel dynamic shared memory alignment tracking.
171	    pub dynamic_smem_alignments: DynamicSmemAlignmentMap,
172	}
173	
174	fn is_mir_or_nvvm_op(ctx: &Context, op: Ptr<Operation>) -> bool {
175	    let opid = Operation::get_opid(op, ctx);
176	    let dialect = opid.dialect.to_string();
177	    dialect == "mir" || dialect == "nvvm"
178	}
179	
180	/// True for a `builtin.constant` whose result is a signed/unsigned (non-signless)
181	/// integer. That is the only `builtin.constant` lowering must touch: `sccp` can
182	/// materialise such a constant (it carries the MIR integer type, e.g. `ui32`), and
183	/// lowering must normalise it to a signless LLVM integer like it does for
184	/// `mir.constant`, else the LLVM module ends up with mismatched operand types
185	/// (a signless op fed by a signed/unsigned constant). A *signless* builtin.constant
186	/// is left alone (legal; the textual exporter emits it). Because the conversion
187	/// emits a signless constant, this predicate is false for the result, so the
188	/// DialectConversion worklist converges instead of looping.
189	fn is_signed_builtin_constant(ctx: &Context, op: Ptr<Operation>) -> bool {
190	    if Operation::get_opid(op, ctx) != pliron::builtin::ops::ConstantOp::get_opid_static() {
191	        return false;
192	    }
193	    let res_ty = op.deref(ctx).get_result(0).get_type(ctx);
194	    res_ty
195	        .deref(ctx)
196	        .downcast_ref::<IntegerType>()
197	        .is_some_and(|it| it.signedness() != Signedness::Signless)
198	}
199	
200	impl DialectConversion for MirToLlvmConversionDriver {
201	    fn can_convert_op(&self, ctx: &Context, op: Ptr<Operation>) -> bool {
202	        // A signless `builtin.constant` is left alone: it is legal and the textual
203	        // exporter emits it directly. But sccp can materialise a builtin.constant
204	        // carrying a signed/unsigned MIR integer type; lowering must normalise that
205	        // to signless (like `mir.constant`), or the LLVM module gets mismatched
206	        // operand types. So mark ONLY a non-signless builtin.constant convertible.
207	        // Its conversion emits a signless constant (no longer convertible), so this
208	        // converges — it does NOT loop the way marking *every* builtin.constant did.
209	        is_mir_or_nvvm_op(ctx, op) || is_signed_builtin_constant(ctx, op)
210	    }
211	
212	    fn can_convert_type(&self, ctx: &Context, ty: TypeHandle) -> bool {
213	        let ty_ref = ty.deref(ctx);
214	
215	        // Signed/unsigned integers need signless normalisation (LLVM convention).
216	        if let Some(int_ty) = ty_ref.downcast_ref::<IntegerType>() {
217	            return int_ty.signedness() != Signedness::Signless;
218	        }
219	
220	        type_impls::<dyn MirConvertibleType>(&*ty_ref)
221	    }
222	
223	    fn convert_type(&mut self, ctx: &mut Context, ty: TypeHandle) -> Result<TypeHandle> {
224	        convert_type(ctx, ty).map_err(|e| pliron::input_error_noloc!("{e}"))
225	    }
226	
227	    fn rewrite(
228	        &mut self,
229	        ctx: &mut Context,
230	        rewriter: &mut DialectConversionRewriter,
231	        op: Ptr<Operation>,
232	        operands_info: &OperandsInfo,
233	    ) -> Result<()> {
234	        let opid = Operation::get_opid(op, ctx);
235	        let loc = op.deref(ctx).loc();
236	
237	        // Special-case ops that need CUDA pass-level state.
238	        if opid == dialect_mir::ops::MirFuncOp::get_opid_static() {
239	            return lowering::convert_func(
240	                ctx,
241	                rewriter,
242	                op,
243	                operands_info,
244	                &mut self.shared_globals,
245	                &mut self.dynamic_smem_alignments,
246	            );
247	        }
248	        if opid == dialect_mir::ops::MirSharedAllocOp::get_opid_static() {
249	            return convert::ops::memory::convert_shared_alloc_dc(
250	                ctx,
251	                rewriter,
252	                op,
253	                operands_info,
254	                &mut self.shared_globals,
255	            );
256	        }
257	        if opid == dialect_mir::ops::MirGlobalAllocOp::get_opid_static() {
258	            return convert::ops::memory::convert_global_alloc_dc(
259	                ctx,
260	                rewriter,
261	                op,
262	                operands_info,
263	                &mut self.device_globals,
264	            );
265	        }
266	        if opid == dialect_mir::ops::MirExternSharedOp::get_opid_static() {
267	            return convert::ops::memory::convert_extern_shared_dc(
268	                ctx,
269	                rewriter,
270	                op,
271	                operands_info,
272	                &mut self.shared_globals,
273	                &mut self.dynamic_smem_alignments,
274	            );
275	        }
276	
277	        // Generic dispatch for all other ops via op_cast.
278	        let op_obj = Operation::get_op_dyn(op, ctx);
279	        let Some(converter) = op_cast::<dyn MirToLlvmConversionInterface>(op_obj.as_ref()) else {
280	            return pliron::input_err!(
281	                loc,
282	                "Unsupported MIR/NVVM op for lowering: {}",
283	                Operation::get_opid(op, ctx)
284	            );
285	        };
286	        converter.convert(ctx, rewriter, operands_info)
287	    }
288	}
289	
290	/// Runs the `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect lowering pass on the given module.
291	///
292	/// This is the main entry point for the lowering pass. It uses pliron's
293	/// `DialectConversion` framework to walk the IR, convert types, and
294	/// dispatch per-op conversion logic.
295	///
296	/// # Arguments
297	///
298	/// * `ctx` - Mutable reference to the pliron context
299	/// * `module_op` - Pointer to the module operation to transform
300	///
301	/// # Returns
302	///
303	/// `Ok(())` if all operations were successfully converted.
304	pub fn lower_mir_to_llvm(ctx: &mut Context, module_op: Ptr<Operation>) -> Result<()> {
305	    let mut conversion = MirToLlvmConversionDriver {
306	        shared_globals: FxHashMap::default(),
307	        device_globals: FxHashMap::default(),
308	        dynamic_smem_alignments: FxHashMap::default(),
309	    };
310	    // pliron's DialectConversion now reports an IRStatus (Changed/Unchanged);
311	    // lowering only cares about success, so discard it.
312	    apply_dialect_conversion(ctx, &mut conversion, module_op)?;
313	    Ok(())
314	}
315

6	//! # Device Code Generation via cuda-oxide Pipeline
7	//!
8	//! This module bridges rustc's internal MIR representation to cuda-oxide's
9	//! existing MIR→PTX pipeline using `rustc_public::rustc_internal` to convert
10	//! between internal and stable_mir types.
11	//!
12	//! ## The Bridge Problem
13	//!
14	//! We have two different MIR representations:
15	//!
16	//! | API                         | Used By                       | Type                                |
17	//! |-----------------------------|-------------------------------|-------------------------------------|
18	//! | `rustc_middle` (internal)   | rustc internals, this backend | `rustc_middle::ty::Instance<'tcx>`  |
19	//! | `rustc_public` (stable MIR) | mir-importer pipeline         | `rustc_public::mir::mono::Instance` |
20	//!
21	//! The cuda-oxide pipeline (mir-importer) was built using `rustc_public` APIs because
22	//! they're more stable. But as a codegen backend, we receive `rustc_middle` types from
23	//! rustc. This module bridges between them.
24	//!
25	//! ## Bridge Architecture
26	//!
27	//! ```text
28	//! ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
29	//! │                         DEVICE CODE GENERATION                                  │
30	//! │                                                                                 │
31	//! │   Input: Vec<CollectedFunction<'tcx>>                                           │
32	//! │          (using rustc_middle::ty::Instance)                                     │
33	//! │                                                                                 │
34	//! │   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
35	//! │   │  STEP 1: Enter stable_mir Context                                       │   │
36	//! │   │                                                                         │   │
37	//! │   │  rustc_internal::run(tcx, || { ... })                                   │   │
38	//! │   │                                                                         │   │
39	//! │   │  This sets up the Tables and CompilerCtxt that enable type conversion   │   │
40	//! │   │  between rustc_middle and rustc_public types.                           │   │
41	//! │   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
42	//! │                              │                                                  │
43	//! │                              ▼                                                  │
44	//! │   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
45	//! │   │  STEP 2: Convert Instances                                              │   │
46	//! │   │                                                                         │   │
47	//! │   │  for each CollectedFunction<'tcx>:                                      │   │
48	//! │   │      stable_instance = rustc_internal::stable(func.instance)            │   │
49	//! │   │                                                                         │   │
50	//! │   │  This converts:                                                         │   │
51	//! │   │    rustc_middle::ty::Instance<'tcx>                                     │   │
52	//! │   │         ▼                                                               │   │
53	//! │   │    rustc_public::mir::mono::Instance                                    │   │
54	//! │   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
55	//! │                              │                                                  │
56	//! │                              ▼                                                  │
57	//! │   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
58	//! │   │  STEP 3: Run cuda-oxide Pipeline                                        │   │
59	//! │   │                                                                         │   │
60	//! │   │  mir_importer::run_pipeline(&stable_functions, &config)                 │   │
61	//! │   │                                                                         │   │
62	//! │   │  Pipeline stages:                                                       │   │
63	//! │   │    1. Rust MIR → `dialect-mir` (alloca form)                            │   │
64	//! │   │    2. `dialect-mir` → `dialect-mir` (mem2reg → SSA)                     │   │
65	//! │   │    3. Apply annotated loop unrolling                                    │   │
66	//! │   │    4. `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect (via `mir-lower`)                    │   │
67	//! │   │    5. LLVM dialect → textual LLVM IR (.ll)                              │   │
68	//! │   │    6. LLVM IR → PTX via `llc` (.ptx)                                    │   │
69	//! │   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
70	//! │                              │                                                  │
71	//! │                              ▼                                                  │
72	//! │   ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐   │
73	//! │   │  Output: DeviceCodegenResult                                            │   │
74	//! │   │                                                                         │   │
75	//! │   │    - ptx_path: Path to generated .ptx file                              │   │
76	//! │   │    - ll_path: Path to generated .ll file                                │   │
77	//! │   │    - target: GPU target (e.g., "sm_80", "sm_90a")                       │   │
78	//! │   │    - ptx_content: PTX as string, when PTX was generated                 │   │
79	//! │   └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘   │
80	//! │                                                                                 │
81	//! └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
82	//! ```
83	//!
84	//! ## Why This Design?
85	//!
86	//! We chose to bridge to stable_mir rather than rewrite mir-importer because:
87	//!
88	//! 1. **Code reuse**: mir-importer already works and is well-tested
89	//! 2. **Stability**: rustc_public APIs change less than rustc internals
90	//! 3. **Simplicity**: ~100 lines of bridge code vs rewriting the pipeline
91	//! 4. **Maintainability**: Changes to mir-importer automatically work here
92	//!
93	//! The cost is one extra type conversion step, but this happens once per function
94	//! and is negligible compared to actual compilation time.
95

98	    DebugInlinedScope, DebugSourcePosition, DebugSourceScope, DebugSourceScopeLocation,
99	    DebugSourceScopeMap,
100	};

107	#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
108	enum DeviceExternTypePosition {
109	    Parameter,
110	    Result,
111	    Pointee,
112	}
113	
114	/// Convert a Rust device-extern type to the LLVM type supported at the
115	/// external function boundary.
116	///
117	/// Raw-pointer pointees are preserved recursively. Unsupported C ABI types
118	/// return an error instead of being treated as an arbitrary pointer. Small
119	/// integer parameters are rejected until their `signext` or `zeroext`
120	/// attributes can be emitted.
121	fn rustc_ty_to_device_extern_type<'tcx>(
122	    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,
123	    ty: Ty<'tcx>,
124	    position: DeviceExternTypePosition,
125	) -> Result<mir_importer::DeviceExternType, String> {
126	    use mir_importer::DeviceExternType as E;
127	
128	    if ty.is_c_void(tcx) {
129	        return if position == DeviceExternTypePosition::Pointee {
130	            // LLVM spells a C void pointer as `i8*` in typed-pointer IR.
131	            Ok(E::Integer(8))
132	        } else {
133	            Err("`c_void` is only supported behind a pointer".to_string())
134	        };
135	    }
136	
137	    let integer = |bits| {
138	        if position == DeviceExternTypePosition::Pointee || matches!(bits, 32 | 64) {
139	            Ok(E::Integer(bits))
140	        } else {
141	            Err(format!(
142	                "`i{bits}` is not yet supported by value in a device extern; use i32/i64 or pass a pointer"
143	            ))
144	        }
145	    };
146	
147	    match ty.kind() {
148	        TyKind::Int(int_ty) => match int_ty {
149	            rustc_middle::ty::IntTy::I8 => integer(8),
150	            rustc_middle::ty::IntTy::I16 => integer(16),
151	            rustc_middle::ty::IntTy::I32 => integer(32),
152	            rustc_middle::ty::IntTy::I64 => integer(64),
153	            rustc_middle::ty::IntTy::I128 => integer(128),
154	            rustc_middle::ty::IntTy::Isize => integer(64), // nvptx64
155	        },
156	        TyKind::Uint(uint_ty) => match uint_ty {
157	            rustc_middle::ty::UintTy::U8 => integer(8),
158	            rustc_middle::ty::UintTy::U16 => integer(16),
159	            rustc_middle::ty::UintTy::U32 => integer(32),
160	            rustc_middle::ty::UintTy::U64 => integer(64),
161	            rustc_middle::ty::UintTy::U128 => integer(128),
162	            rustc_middle::ty::UintTy::Usize => integer(64), // nvptx64
163	        },
164	        TyKind::Float(float_ty) => match float_ty {
165	            rustc_middle::ty::FloatTy::F16 if position == DeviceExternTypePosition::Pointee => {
166	                Ok(E::Float16)
167	            }
168	            rustc_middle::ty::FloatTy::F16 => Err(
169	                "`f16` is not yet supported by value in a device extern; pass a pointer or use a CUDA C wrapper".to_string(),
170	            ),
171	            rustc_middle::ty::FloatTy::F32 => Ok(E::Float32),
172	            rustc_middle::ty::FloatTy::F64 => Ok(E::Float64),
173	            rustc_middle::ty::FloatTy::F128 => {
174	                Err("f128 device externs are not supported".to_string())
175	            }
176	        },
177	        TyKind::RawPtr(pointee, _) | TyKind::Ref(_, pointee, _) => {
178	            let pointee = if matches!(pointee.kind(), TyKind::Tuple(fields) if fields.is_empty()) {
179	                // Rust's `*mut ()` is its common spelling for a void pointer.
180	                E::Integer(8)
181	            } else {
182	                rustc_ty_to_device_extern_type(
183	                    tcx,
184	                    *pointee,
185	                    DeviceExternTypePosition::Pointee,
186	                )?
187	            };
188	            Ok(E::pointer_to(pointee, 0))
189	        }
190	        TyKind::Array(element, len) if position == DeviceExternTypePosition::Pointee => {
191	            let len = len.try_to_target_usize(tcx).ok_or_else(|| {
192	                format!("device-extern array length for `{ty}` is not a concrete constant")
193	            })?;
194	            let element = rustc_ty_to_device_extern_type(
195	                tcx,
196	                *element,
197	                DeviceExternTypePosition::Pointee,
198	            )?;
199	            Ok(E::Array {
200	                element: Box::new(element),
201	                len,
202	            })
203	        }
204	        TyKind::Tuple(fields)
205	            if fields.is_empty() && position == DeviceExternTypePosition::Result =>
206	        {
207	            Ok(E::Void)
208	        }
209	        TyKind::Bool => Err(
210	            "`bool` is not yet supported in device extern signatures; use `u32` in a C-compatible wrapper"
211	                .to_string(),
212	        ),
213	        TyKind::Char => Err(
214	            "Rust `char` is not supported in device extern signatures; use `u32` in a C-compatible wrapper".to_string(),
215	        ),
216	        TyKind::Never => Err("never-returning device externs are not yet supported".to_string()),
217	        _ => Err(format!(
218	            "unsupported device-extern ABI type `{ty}`; use scalar C types or raw pointers to supported scalar/array pointees"
219	        )),
220	    }
221	}

292	fn build_debug_source_scope_map<'tcx>(
293	    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,
294	    func: &CollectedFunction<'tcx>,
295	) -> DebugSourceScopeMap {
296	    let mir = tcx.instance_mir(func.instance.def);
297	    let scopes = mir
298	        .source_scopes
299	        .iter_enumerated()
300	        .map(|(scope, data)| {
301	            let inlined = data.inlined.map(|(callee, callsite)| {
302	                let callee = tcx.instantiate_and_normalize_erasing_regions(
303	                    func.instance.args,
304	                    TypingEnv::fully_monomorphized(),
305	                    EarlyBinder::bind(callee),
306	                );
307	                let callsite = hygiene::walk_chain_collapsed(callsite, mir.span);
308	                DebugInlinedScope {
309	                    callee_name: rustc_middle::ty::print::with_no_trimmed_paths!(
310	                        callee.to_string()
311	                    ),
312	                    callsite: debug_position_from_span(tcx, callsite),
313	                }
314	            });
315	
316	            DebugSourceScope {
317	                id: scope.as_u32(),
318	                parent: data.parent_scope.map(|parent| parent.as_u32()),
319	                span: debug_position_from_span(tcx, data.span.source_callsite()),
320	                inlined,
321	            }
322	        })
323	        .collect();
324	
325	    let mut locations = Vec::new();
326	    for block in mir.basic_blocks.iter() {
327	        for stmt in &block.statements {
328	            if let Some(pos) = debug_position_from_span(tcx, stmt.source_info.span) {
329	                locations.push(DebugSourceScopeLocation {
330	                    pos,
331	                    scope: stmt.source_info.scope.as_u32(),
332	                });
333	            }
334	        }
335	
336	        let terminator = block.terminator();
337	        if let Some(pos) = debug_position_from_span(tcx, terminator.source_info.span) {
338	            locations.push(DebugSourceScopeLocation {
339	                pos,
340	                scope: terminator.source_info.scope.as_u32(),
341	            });
342	        }
343	    }
344	    locations.sort_by(|lhs, rhs| {
345	        (&lhs.pos.file, lhs.pos.line, lhs.pos.column, lhs.scope).cmp(&(
346	            &rhs.pos.file,
347	            rhs.pos.line,
348	            rhs.pos.column,
349	            rhs.scope,
350	        ))
351	    });
352	    locations.dedup();
353	
354	    DebugSourceScopeMap { scopes, locations }
355	}

357	fn debug_position_from_span(tcx: TyCtxt<'_>, span: Span) -> Option<DebugSourcePosition> {
358	    let (file, line, column, _, _) = tcx.sess.source_map().span_to_location_info(span);
359	    let file = file?;
360	    if line == 0 || column == 0 {
361	        return None;
362	    }
363	
364	    Some(DebugSourcePosition {
365	        file: file.name.prefer_local_unconditionally().to_string().into(),
366	        line: line as i32,
367	        column: column as i32,
368	    })
369	}

411	/// Generates PTX for device functions using the cuda-oxide pipeline.
412	///
413	/// This is the main entry point for device codegen. It bridges between
414	/// rustc's internal types (`rustc_middle`) and mir-importer's stable_mir-based
415	/// pipeline (`rustc_public`).
416	///
417	/// ## Parameters
418	///
419	/// - `tcx`: The type context from rustc
420	/// - `functions`: Collected device functions from the collector module
421	/// - `config`: Output and diagnostic configuration
422	///
423	/// ## Returns
424	///
425	/// - `Ok(DeviceCodegenResult)`: Paths to generated .ll and .ptx files
426	/// - `Err(DeviceCodegenError)`: Description of what went wrong
427	///
428	/// ## Pipeline Stages
429	///
430	/// ```text
431	/// CollectedFunction<'tcx>
432	///         │
433	///         ├──▶ rustc_internal::stable() ──▶ rustc_public::Instance
434	///         │
435	///         └──▶ mir_importer::run_pipeline()
436	///                     │
437	///                     ├──▶ `dialect-mir` (alloca form)
438	///                     │
439	///                     ├──▶ `dialect-mir` (mem2reg → SSA)
440	///                     ├──▶ annotated loop unroll
441	///                     │
442	///                     ├──▶ LLVM dialect
443	///                     │
444	///                     ├──▶ textual LLVM IR (.ll)
445	///                     │
446	///                     └──▶ PTX (.ptx) via `llc`
447	/// ```
448	pub fn generate_device_code<'tcx>(
449	    tcx: TyCtxt<'tcx>,
450	    functions: &[CollectedFunction<'tcx>],
451	    device_externs: &[DeviceExternDecl],
452	    config: &DeviceCodegenConfig,
453	) -> Result<DeviceCodegenResult, DeviceCodegenError> {
454	    use rustc_public::rustc_internal;
455	
456	    if functions.is_empty() {
457	        return Err(DeviceCodegenError::NoKernels);
458	    }
459	
460	    if config.verbose {
461	        eprintln!(
462	            "[device_codegen] Compiling {} functions, {} device externs to PTX via cuda-oxide pipeline",
463	            functions.len(),
464	            device_externs.len()
465	        );
466	        for func in functions {
467	            eprintln!(
468	                "[device_codegen]   {} {}",
469	                if func.is_kernel { "kernel" } else { "device" },
470	                func.export_name
471	            );
472	        }
473	        for decl in device_externs {
474	            eprintln!(
475	                "[device_codegen]   extern {} (convergent={}, pure={}, readonly={})",
476	                decl.export_name,
477	                decl.attrs.is_convergent,
478	                decl.attrs.is_pure,
479	                decl.attrs.is_readonly
480	            );
481	        }
482	    }
483	
484	    // Prepare data we need to pass into the stable_mir closure
485	    // (closures can't capture references to local TyCtxt data)
486	    let export_names: Vec<(String, bool)> = functions
487	        .iter()
488	        .map(|f| (f.export_name.clone(), f.is_kernel))
489	        .collect();
490	    let debug_scope_maps: Vec<_> = functions
491	        .iter()
492	        .map(|f| build_debug_source_scope_map(tcx, f))
493	        .collect();
494	
495	    // Convert device externs to mir-importer format
496	    // We extract signature info from rustc here since we have access to TyCtxt
497	    let stable_device_externs: Vec<mir_importer::DeviceExternDecl> = device_externs
498	        .iter()
499	        .map(|decl| {
500	            // Get function signature from rustc
501	            let fn_sig = tcx.fn_sig(decl.def_id).instantiate_identity();
502	            let fn_sig = fn_sig.skip_binder();
503	
504	            if !matches!(fn_sig.abi, rustc_abi::ExternAbi::C { unwind: false }) {
505	                return Err(DeviceCodegenError::InvalidDeviceExternSignature(format!(
506	                    "`{}` uses ABI {:?}; device externs must use `extern \"C\"` without unwinding",
507	                    decl.export_name, fn_sig.abi
508	                )));
509	            }
510	
511	            if fn_sig.c_variadic {
512	                return Err(DeviceCodegenError::InvalidDeviceExternSignature(format!(
513	                    "`{}` is variadic; variadic device externs are not supported",
514	                    decl.export_name
515	                )));
516	            }
517	
518	            let param_types = fn_sig
519	                .inputs()
520	                .iter()
521	                .enumerate()
522	                .map(|(index, ty)| {
523	                    rustc_ty_to_device_extern_type(tcx, *ty, DeviceExternTypePosition::Parameter)
524	                        .map_err(|reason| {
525	                            DeviceCodegenError::InvalidDeviceExternSignature(format!(
526	                                "`{}` parameter {} (`{ty}`): {reason}",
527	                                decl.export_name, index
528	                            ))
529	                        })
530	                })
531	                .collect::<Result<Vec<_>, _>>()?;
532	
533	            let result_ty = fn_sig.output();
534	            let return_type =
535	                rustc_ty_to_device_extern_type(tcx, result_ty, DeviceExternTypePosition::Result)
536	                    .map_err(|reason| {
537	                        DeviceCodegenError::InvalidDeviceExternSignature(format!(
538	                            "`{}` result (`{result_ty}`): {reason}",
539	                            decl.export_name
540	                        ))
541	                    })?;
542	
543	            Ok(mir_importer::DeviceExternDecl {
544	                export_name: decl.export_name.clone(),
545	                param_types,
546	                return_type,
547	                attrs: mir_importer::DeviceExternAttrs {
548	                    is_convergent: decl.attrs.is_convergent,
549	                    is_pure: decl.attrs.is_pure,
550	                    is_readonly: decl.attrs.is_readonly,
551	                },
552	            })
553	        })
554	        .collect::<Result<_, _>>()?;
555	
556	    let output_dir = config.output_dir.clone();
557	    let output_name = config.output_name.clone();
558	    let verbose = config.verbose;
559	    let show_rustc_mir = config.dump_rustc_mir;
560	    let show_mir = config.dump_mir_dialect;
561	    let show_llvm = config.dump_llvm_dialect;
562	
563	    // Print raw rustc MIR if requested (before conversion to stable_mir)
564	    if show_rustc_mir {
565	        use rustc_middle::ty::print::with_no_trimmed_paths;
566	
567	        eprintln!();
568	        eprintln!("=== Rustc MIR (before translation) ===");
569	        for func in functions {
570	            let mir = tcx.instance_mir(func.instance.def);
571	            eprintln!();
572	            eprintln!("fn {} {{", func.export_name);
573	
574	            // Print locals
575	            eprintln!(
576	                "    let mut _0: {:?};",
577	                mir.local_decls[rustc_middle::mir::Local::from_u32(0)].ty
578	            );
579	            for (local, decl) in mir.local_decls.iter_enumerated().skip(1) {
580	                let mutability = if decl.mutability == rustc_middle::mir::Mutability::Mut {
581	                    "mut "
582	                } else {
583	                    ""
584	                };
585	                eprintln!("    let {}_{}:  {:?};", mutability, local.index(), decl.ty);
586	            }
587	
588	            // Print debug info
589	            for debug_info in &mir.var_debug_info {
590	                with_no_trimmed_paths!(eprintln!(
591	                    "    debug {:?} => {:?};",
592	                    debug_info.name, debug_info.value
593	                ));
594	            }
595	
596	            // Print basic blocks
597	            for (bb_idx, bb_data) in mir.basic_blocks.iter_enumerated() {
598	                eprintln!("    bb{}: {{", bb_idx.index());
599	                for stmt in &bb_data.statements {
600	                    with_no_trimmed_paths!(eprintln!("        {:?}", stmt));
601	                }
602	                with_no_trimmed_paths!(eprintln!("        {:?}", bb_data.terminator().kind));
603	                eprintln!("    }}");
604	            }
605	            eprintln!("}}");
606	        }
607	        eprintln!();
608	    }
609	
610	    // Enter stable_mir context and run the pipeline.
611	    //
612	    // rustc_internal::run() does the following:
613	    // 1. Creates Tables for type/instance interning
614	    // 2. Sets up thread-local CompilerCtxt
615	    // 3. Runs our closure with access to stable() conversion
616	    // 4. Tears down the context and returns our result
617	    // Pre-compute `#[inline(always)]` flags before entering the stable_mir
618	    // context, since the query lives on `rustc_middle::TyCtxt` and is not
619	    // exposed through stable_mir. Preserving this hint avoids making helper
620	    // boundaries depend entirely on later optimizer heuristics.
621	    let inline_always_flags: Vec<bool> = functions
622	        .iter()
623	        .map(|func| {
624	            let def_id = func.instance.def_id();
625	            matches!(
626	                tcx.codegen_fn_attrs(def_id).inline,
627	                rustc_hir::attrs::InlineAttr::Always | rustc_hir::attrs::InlineAttr::Force { .. }
628	            )
629	        })
630	        .collect();
631	
632	    let result = rustc_internal::run(tcx, || {
633	        // Convert internal Instance<'tcx> to stable_mir Instance
634	        let stable_functions: Vec<mir_importer::CollectedFunction> = functions
635	            .iter()
636	            .zip(export_names.iter())
637	            .zip(debug_scope_maps.iter())
638	            .zip(inline_always_flags.iter())
639	            .map(
640	                |(((func, (export_name, is_kernel)), debug_source_scopes), is_inline_always)| {
641	                    // Use rustc_internal::stable() to convert the Instance.
642	                    // This is the key bridge between rustc_middle and rustc_public types.
643	                    let stable_instance = rustc_internal::stable(func.instance);
644	
645	                    mir_importer::CollectedFunction {
646	                        instance: stable_instance,
647	                        is_kernel: *is_kernel,
648	                        export_name: export_name.clone(),
649	                        debug_source_scopes: Some(debug_source_scopes.clone()),
650	                        is_inline_always: *is_inline_always,
651	                    }
652	                },
653	            )
654	            .collect();
655	
656	        // Check for NVVM IR mode (set by cargo oxide --emit-nvvm-ir)
657	        let emit_nvvm_ir = std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_EMIT_NVVM_IR").is_ok();
658	
659	        if verbose {
660	            eprintln!(
661	                "[device_codegen] Converted {} functions to stable_mir format",
662	                stable_functions.len()
663	            );
664	            if emit_nvvm_ir {
665	                eprintln!("[device_codegen] NVVM IR mode enabled");
666	            }
667	        }
668	
669	        let debug_kind = device_debug_kind(tcx.sess.opts.debuginfo);
670	        let target_arch = std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET").ok();
671	        let device_arch_hint = std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_DEVICE_ARCH").ok();
672	
673	        // Create pipeline config
674	        let pipeline_config = mir_importer::PipelineConfig {
675	            output_dir: output_dir.clone(),
676	            output_name: output_name.clone(),
677	            verbose,
678	            show_mir_dialect: show_mir,
679	            show_llvm_dialect: show_llvm,
680	            emit_nvvm_ir,
681	            target_arch,
682	            device_arch_hint,
683	            debug_kind,
684	        };
685	
686	        // Run the cuda-oxide pipeline!
687	        // Rust MIR → `dialect-mir` → mem2reg → unroll → LLVM dialect → LLVM IR → PTX.
688	        // Device externs are emitted as `declare` statements in LLVM IR
689	        mir_importer::run_pipeline(&stable_functions, &stable_device_externs, &pipeline_config)
690	    });

692	    // Handle the result from rustc_internal::run.
693	    // We have nested Results: outer from run(), inner from run_pipeline().
694	    match result {
695	        Ok(pipeline_result) => match pipeline_result {
696	            Ok(compilation_result) => {
697	                let artifact = read_compilation_artifact(&compilation_result)?;
698	                let ptx_content = match artifact.as_ref() {
699	                    Some(artifact) if artifact.kind == DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::Ptx => {
700	                        Some(String::from_utf8(artifact.bytes.clone()).map_err(|e| {
701	                            DeviceCodegenError::PtxGeneration(format!(
702	                                "generated PTX is not valid UTF-8: {e}"
703	                            ))
704	                        })?)
705	                    }
706	                    _ => None,
707	                };
708	
709	                if config.verbose {
710	                    if let Some(artifact) = artifact.as_ref() {
711	                        eprintln!(
712	                            "[device_codegen] Embeddable artifact generated: {} ({:?}, target: {})",
713	                            artifact.name, artifact.kind, compilation_result.target
714	                        );
715	                    } else {
716	                        eprintln!(
717	                            "[device_codegen] No embeddable artifact found for {} (target: {})",
718	                            compilation_result.ll_path.display(),
719	                            compilation_result.target
720	                        );
721	                    }
722	                }
723	
724	                Ok(DeviceCodegenResult {
725	                    ptx_path: compilation_result.ptx_path,
726	                    ll_path: compilation_result.ll_path,
727	                    target: compilation_result.target,
728	                    ptx_content,
729	                    artifact,
730	                })
731	            }
732	            Err(pipeline_err) => Err(DeviceCodegenError::PtxGeneration(format!(
733	                "{}",
734	                pipeline_err
735	            ))),
736	        },
737	        Err(stable_mir_err) => Err(DeviceCodegenError::StableMirError(format!(
738	            "{:?}",
739	            stable_mir_err
740	        ))),
741	    }
742	}

744	fn device_debug_kind(rustc_debug: DebugInfo) -> llvm_export::export::DebugKind {
745	    device_debug_kind_with_override(
746	        rustc_debug,
747	        std::env::var("CUDA_OXIDE_DEBUG").ok().as_deref(),
748	    )
749	}
750	
751	fn device_debug_kind_with_override(
752	    rustc_debug: DebugInfo,
753	    override_value: Option<&str>,
754	) -> llvm_export::export::DebugKind {
755	    if let Some(value) = override_value {
756	        match value.trim().to_ascii_lowercase().as_str() {
757	            "0" | "off" | "none" => return llvm_export::export::DebugKind::Off,
758	            "1" | "line" | "lines" | "line-tables" | "line-tables-only" => {
759	                return llvm_export::export::DebugKind::LineTables;
760	            }
761	            "2" | "full" => return llvm_export::export::DebugKind::Full,
762	            _ => {}
763	        }
764	    }
765	
766	    match rustc_debug {
767	        DebugInfo::None => llvm_export::export::DebugKind::Off,
768	        DebugInfo::LineDirectivesOnly
769	        | DebugInfo::LineTablesOnly
770	        | DebugInfo::Limited
771	        | DebugInfo::Full => llvm_export::export::DebugKind::LineTables,
772	    }
773	}
774	
775	fn read_compilation_artifact(
776	    result: &mir_importer::CompilationResult,
777	) -> Result<Option<DeviceCodegenArtifact>, DeviceCodegenError> {
778	    let kind = match result.artifact_kind {
779	        mir_importer::CompilationArtifactKind::Ptx => DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::Ptx,
780	        mir_importer::CompilationArtifactKind::NvvmIr => DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::NvvmIr,
781	        mir_importer::CompilationArtifactKind::Ltoir => DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::Ltoir,
782	        mir_importer::CompilationArtifactKind::Cubin => DeviceCodegenArtifactKind::Cubin,
783	    };
784	
785	    match std::fs::read(&result.artifact_path) {
786	        Ok(bytes) => Ok(Some(DeviceCodegenArtifact {
787	            kind,
788	            name: result
789	                .artifact_path
790	                .file_name()
791	                .and_then(|name| name.to_str())
792	                .unwrap_or("device-artifact")
793	                .to_string(),
794	            bytes,
795	        })),
796	        Err(e) if e.kind() == std::io::ErrorKind::NotFound => Ok(None),
797	        Err(e) => Err(DeviceCodegenError::Io(e)),
798	    }
799	}

6	//! Compilation pipeline: MIR → `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect → LLVM IR → PTX.
7	//!
8	//! Orchestrates the full compilation flow from collected MIR functions to
9	//! executable PTX code.
10	//!
11	//! # Pipeline Steps
12	//!
13	//! ```text
14	//! MIR -> dialect-mir -> verify -> mem2reg -> annotated loop unroll
15	//!     -> LLVM dialect -> LLVM IR -> PTX
16	//! ```
17	//!
18	//! Builds with variable debug information skip `mem2reg` and loop unrolling so
19	//! source variables remain in stable stack slots.
20	//!
21	//! # GPU Target Selection
22	//!
23	//! The pipeline auto-detects GPU features in the generated LLVM IR and selects
24	//! an appropriate target:
25	//!
26	//! | Feature                       | Target  | Architecture         |
27	//! |-------------------------------|---------|----------------------|
28	//! | tcgen05/TMEM, CTA-group TMA   | sm_100a | Blackwell datacenter |
29	//! | CLC / newer generic TMA       | sm_100  | Blackwell+           |
30	//! | CLC multicast / accel. TMA    | sm_100a | Blackwell family     |
31	//! | PTX 8.6 matrix shapes/types   | sm_100a | Blackwell family     |
32	//! | TMA multicast                 | sm_100a | sm_90+ (a/f advised) |
33	//! | WGMMA                         | sm_90a  | Hopper only          |
34	//! | `stmatrix.m8n8.b16`          | sm_90   | PTX 7.8+             |
35	//! | TMA/mbarrier                  | sm_100  | Hopper+ compatible   |
36	//! | bf16x2 add/sub/mul            | sm_90   | Hopper+ compatible   |
37	//! | other bf16x2 ALU              | sm_80   | Ampere+ compatible   |
38	//! | `cp.async` (non-bulk)         | sm_80   | Ampere+              |
39	//! | `movmatrix.m8n8.b16`          | sm_75   | PTX 7.8+             |
40	//! | `ldmatrix.m8n8.b16`           | sm_75   | PTX 6.5+             |
41	//! | Basic CUDA                    | sm_80   | Ampere+ (max compat) |
42	//!
43	//! Override with `CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET=<target>` environment variable.
44

51	/// A function collected for GPU compilation.
52	///
53	/// Represents a monomorphized function instance that will be translated to PTX.
54	/// For generic functions like `add::<f32>`, the instance contains the concrete
55	/// type substitutions.
56	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
57	pub struct CollectedFunction {
58	    /// The monomorphized stable_mir instance (includes concrete generic args).
59	    pub instance: Instance,
60	    /// True if this is a GPU kernel entry point (has `#[kernel]` attribute).
61	    pub is_kernel: bool,
62	    /// The name to export in PTX. For kernels, this is the user-visible name.
63	    pub export_name: String,
64	    /// rustc MIR source-scope data used to build inlined debug scopes.
65	    pub debug_source_scopes: Option<llvm_export::ops::DebugSourceScopeMap>,
66	    /// True if the function is marked `#[inline(always)]` in rustc's
67	    /// `CodegenFnAttrs`. The stable_mir API does not expose inline hints, so
68	    /// this is queried via `rustc_middle::TyCtxt::codegen_fn_attrs` in
69	    /// `rustc-codegen-cuda` and threaded through.
70	    ///
71	    /// When true, the LLVM `alwaysinline` attribute is emitted on the
72	    /// function definition. The existing matched LLVM middle-end (`opt -O2`),
73	    /// when available, can then honor the attribute before PTX generation;
74	    /// this flag does not add a separate mandatory inliner pass.
75	    ///
76	    /// This preserves Rust's inline intent for device helpers and avoids
77	    /// making helper boundaries depend entirely on later optimizer heuristics.
78	    pub is_inline_always: bool,
79	}

81	/// An external device function declaration (for FFI with external LTOIR).
82	///
83	/// Unlike `CollectedFunction`, these have no MIR body - they're just declarations
84	/// that will be emitted as LLVM `declare` statements for nvJitLink to resolve
85	/// when linking with external LTOIR (e.g., CCCL libraries).
86	#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
87	pub struct DeviceExternDecl {
88	    /// The export name (the original function name, e.g., "cub_block_reduce_sum").
89	    pub export_name: String,
90	
91	    /// Structured LLVM ABI parameter types. Pointer pointees are retained even
92	    /// though the lowered pliron LLVM module itself uses opaque pointers.
93	    pub param_types: Vec<DeviceExternType>,
94	
95	    /// Structured LLVM ABI return type.
96	    pub return_type: DeviceExternType,
97	
98	    /// NVVM attributes for this function.
99	    pub attrs: DeviceExternAttrs,
100	}

102	/// NVVM attributes for device extern declarations.
103	///
104	/// NOTE: These attributes are currently **not emitted** to the LLVM IR output.
105	/// When linking LTOIR via nvJitLink, the external library's LTOIR already contains
106	/// proper attributes (convergent, nounwind, memory, etc.) on the function DEFINITIONS.
107	/// nvJitLink uses the definition's attributes during LTO, making attributes on our
108	/// declarations redundant.
109	///
110	/// This struct is retained for the pipeline API but values are not used in code generation.
111	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Default, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
112	pub struct DeviceExternAttrs {
113	    /// Function is convergent (all threads must execute together).
114	    /// NOTE: Not currently emitted to LLVM IR.
115	    pub is_convergent: bool,
116	
117	    /// Function is pure (no side effects, result depends only on inputs).
118	    /// NOTE: Not currently emitted to LLVM IR.
119	    pub is_pure: bool,
120	
121	    /// Function is read-only (only reads memory, doesn't write).
122	    /// NOTE: Not currently emitted to LLVM IR.
123	    pub is_readonly: bool,
124	}

152	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
153	pub enum CompilationArtifactKind {
154	    /// Textual PTX assembly, loadable by the CUDA driver.
155	    Ptx,
156	    /// NVVM-compatible LLVM IR, intended for libNVVM/nvJitLink.

158	    /// Binary LTOIR, intended for nvJitLink.

183	    pub output_name: String,
184	    /// Print progress messages to stdout.
185	    pub verbose: bool,
186	    /// Dump the `dialect-mir` module after translation (for debugging).
187	    pub show_mir_dialect: bool,
188	    /// Dump the LLVM dialect module after lowering (for debugging).
189	    pub show_llvm_dialect: bool,
190	    /// Emit NVVM IR suitable for libNVVM or other NVVM-compatible tools.
191	    ///
192	    /// When true:
193	    /// - Uses full NVPTX datalayout
194	    /// - Adds `@llvm.used` to preserve kernels from optimization
195	    /// - Adds `!nvvm.annotations` for all kernels
196	    /// - Adds `!nvvmir.version` metadata
197	    /// - Outputs `.ll` file in NVVM IR format
198	    ///
199	    /// The output can be compiled to LTOIR using `nvvmCompileProgram -gen-lto`.
200	    ///
201	    /// Pre-Blackwell targets use the legacy LLVM 7 dialect; Blackwell and
202	    /// newer targets use the modern opaque-pointer dialect. Architecture is
203	    /// controlled by `target_arch` or `device_arch_hint` (normally populated
204	    /// by `cargo oxide`). When an ordinary build switches to NVVM IR after
205	    /// detecting libdevice, the pipeline may instead select the module's
206	    /// feature-based target floor.
207	    pub emit_nvvm_ir: bool,
208	    /// Explicit CUDA target used to choose NVVM IR syntax.
209	    ///
210	    /// Normally set by `cargo oxide --arch` or `CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET`.
211	    pub target_arch: Option<String>,
212	    /// Detected architecture of the local GPU (`CUDA_OXIDE_DEVICE_ARCH`).
213	    ///
214	    /// Used only when no explicit target is provided.
215	    pub device_arch_hint: Option<String>,
216	    /// Device debug metadata tier.
217	    pub debug_kind: DebugKind,

236	/// Runs the full compilation pipeline on collected functions.
237	///
238	/// # Pipeline Steps
239	///
240	/// 1. Register the `dialect-mir`, `dialect-nvvm`, and LLVM dialects
241	/// 2. Translate each function's MIR body into `dialect-mir`
242	/// 3. Verify the `dialect-mir` module
243	/// 4. Unless full variable-debug mode is enabled, run `mem2reg` to promote slot
244	///    allocas back into SSA
245	/// 5. In the same modes, unroll annotated loops and clean up changed functions
246	/// 6. Lower `dialect-mir` → LLVM dialect (via `mir-lower`)
247	/// 7. Verify the LLVM dialect module
248	/// 8. Export the LLVM dialect to a `.ll` file (including device extern declarations)
249	/// 9. Invoke `llc` to generate PTX (or emit LTOIR/NVVM IR when requested)
250	///
251	/// # Target Selection
252	///
253	/// Automatically detects GPU features (WGMMA, TMA, tcgen05) and selects
254	/// an appropriate SM target. Can be overridden via `CUDA_OXIDE_TARGET`.
255	///
256	/// # Device Externs
257	///
258	/// External device function declarations (from `#[device] extern "C" { ... }`)
259	/// are emitted as LLVM `declare` statements. These are resolved at link time
260	/// by nvJitLink when linking with external LTOIR (e.g., CCCL libraries).
261	///
262	/// # Errors
263	///
264	/// Returns [`PipelineError`] with details on which step failed.
265	pub fn run_pipeline(
266	    functions: &[CollectedFunction],
267	    device_externs: &[DeviceExternDecl],
268	    config: &PipelineConfig,
269	) -> Result<CompilationResult, PipelineError> {
270	    prepare_output_dir(&config.output_dir)?;
271	
272	    let mut ctx = Context::new();
273	
274	    // Step 1: Register dialects
275	    crate::translator::register_dialects(&mut ctx);
276	
277	    // Step 2: Create module
278	    let module_name: pliron::identifier::Identifier = config
279	        .output_name
280	        .clone()
281	        .try_into()
282	        .unwrap_or_else(|_| "kernel".try_into().unwrap());
283	    let module = pliron::builtin::ops::ModuleOp::new(&mut ctx, module_name);
284	    let module_op_ptr = module.get_operation();
285	
286	    let mut legaliser = Legaliser::default();
287	
288	    // Step 3: Translate all functions
289	    for func in functions {
290	        if config.verbose {
291	            eprintln!(
292	                "Translating {}: {}",
293	                if func.is_kernel {
294	                    "kernel"
295	                } else {
296	                    "device fn"
297	                },
298	                func.export_name
299	            );
300	        }
301	
302	        let body = func
303	            .instance
304	            .body()
305	            .ok_or_else(|| PipelineError::NoBody(func.export_name.clone()))?;
306	
307	        let func_op_ptr = crate::translator::body::translate_body(
308	            &mut ctx,
309	            &body,
310	            &func.instance,
311	            func.is_kernel,
312	            func.is_inline_always,
313	            Some(&func.export_name),
314	            &mut legaliser,
315	            config.debug_kind,
316	            func.debug_source_scopes.as_ref(),
317	        )
318	        .map_err(|e| {
319	            // Use .disp(&ctx) for rich error formatting with location and backtrace
320	            PipelineError::Translation(format!("{}: {}", func.export_name, e.disp(&ctx)))
321	        })?;
322	
323	        // Dump the per-function IR BEFORE verification so users can see
324	        // what the translator produced even when verification fails. If we
325	        // verified first and bailed, `--show-mir-dialect` / `CUDA_OXIDE_DUMP_MIR`
326	        // would silently print nothing for the offending function.
327	        if config.show_mir_dialect {
328	            eprintln!(
329	                "\n=== dialect-mir func: {} (pre-verify) ===",
330	                func.export_name
331	            );
332	            eprintln!("{}", func_op_ptr.deref(&ctx).disp(&ctx));
333	        }
334	
335	        verify_operation(&ctx, func_op_ptr, &func.export_name)?;
336	
337	        // Append to module
338	        append_to_module(&ctx, module_op_ptr, func_op_ptr);
339	    }
340	
341	    // Step 4: Verify module. Dump BEFORE verify so module-level verification
342	    // failures still surface the consolidated IR to the user.
343	    if config.show_mir_dialect {
344	        eprintln!("\n=== dialect-mir module (pre-verify) ===");
345	        eprintln!("{}", module_op_ptr.deref(&ctx).disp(&ctx));
346	    }
347	    if config.verbose {
348	        eprintln!("\n=== Verifying dialect-mir module ===");
349	    }
350	    verify_operation(&ctx, module_op_ptr, "module")?;
351	    if config.verbose {
352	        eprintln!("dialect-mir verification successful ✓");
353	    }
354	
355	    // Step 4.5: Run mem2reg (promote `mir.alloca` + `mir.load`/`mir.store`
356	    // chains back to SSA values).
357	    //
358	    // Full-debug is a `-G`-style build: we keep every source local in its stack
359	    // slot so cuda-gdb can read it from a stable memory location for the whole
360	    // scope (via `llvm.dbg.declare`). Promoting locals to SSA would narrow each
361	    // variable's inspectable range to its register's liveness, which is why an
362	    // optimized `dbg.value` build shows `<optimized out>` for in-scope locals.
363	    // We therefore skip mem2reg whenever variable info is requested. The
364	    // promotion-aware `mir.dbg_value` salvage (see `dialect-mir::ops::debug`)
365	    // remains the mechanism for any future optimized-debug tier that *does*
366	    // promote.
367	    if config.debug_kind.variables_enabled() {
368	        if config.verbose {
369	            eprintln!("\n=== Skipping mem2reg (full debug keeps locals in memory) ===");
370	        }
371	    } else {
372	        if config.verbose {
373	            eprintln!("\n=== Running mem2reg ===");
374	        }
375	        // pliron's pass infra now threads an AnalysisManager through mem2reg
376	        // (caches dominator trees etc.); we run it standalone, so a fresh empty
377	        // manager suffices. The returned IRStatus (Changed/Unchanged) is discarded.
378	        let mut analyses = pliron::pass_manager::AnalysisManager::default();
379	        pliron::opts::mem2reg::mem2reg(module_op_ptr, &mut ctx, &mut analyses).map_err(|e| {
380	            PipelineError::Verification {
381	                name: "mem2reg".to_string(),
382	                message: e.disp(&ctx).to_string(),
383	                operation: None,
384	            }
385	        })?;
386	        if config.verbose {
387	            eprintln!("mem2reg successful ✓");
388	        }
389	        if config.show_mir_dialect {
390	            eprintln!("\n=== dialect-mir module (after mem2reg) ===");
391	            eprintln!("{}", module_op_ptr.deref(&ctx).disp(&ctx));
392	        }
393	        verify_operation(&ctx, module_op_ptr, "module post-mem2reg")?;
394	
395	        // Step 4.6: annotation-driven loop unrolling (#[unroll] / #[unroll(N)]).
396	        // Runs on the SSA form mem2reg just produced; a no-op unless a loop
397	        // contains a `mir.unroll_hint` operation. The pass receives mem2reg's
398	        // AnalysisManager for the standard pass shape, but recomputes dominance
399	        // after each CFG rewrite.
400	        if config.verbose {
401	            eprintln!("\n=== Running loop-unroll ===");
402	        }
403	        mir_transforms::unroll::unroll_annotated_loops(module_op_ptr, &mut ctx, &mut analyses)
404	            .map_err(|e| PipelineError::Verification {
405	                name: "loop-unroll".to_string(),
406	                message: e.disp(&ctx).to_string(),
407	                operation: None,
408	            })?;
409	        verify_operation(&ctx, module_op_ptr, "module post-unroll")?;
410	        // Constant folding (sccp -> simplify_cfg -> dce) runs inside the unroll
411	        // pass, scoped to functions it actually unrolled; see
412	        // `mir_transforms::unroll`. Non-unrolled kernels are left for `opt`/NVVM.
413	    }
414	
415	    // Step 4.9: Add structured device-extern declarations before call
416	    // lowering. The call converter consults these declarations to preserve
417	    // pointer address spaces and insert an explicit addrspacecast when the
418	    // caller and external ABI differ. Adding declarations only after lowering
419	    // is too late: every unknown pointer argument has already fallen back to
420	    // generic addrspace(0) by then.
421	    if !device_externs.is_empty() {
422	        if config.verbose {
423	            eprintln!(
424	                "\n=== Adding {} device extern declarations ===",
425	                device_externs.len()
426	            );
427	        }
428	        add_device_extern_declarations(&mut ctx, module_op_ptr, device_externs)?;
429	    }
430	
431	    // Step 5: Lower dialect-mir → LLVM dialect.
432	    if config.verbose {
433	        eprintln!("\n=== Lowering dialect-mir → LLVM dialect ===");
434	    }
435	    lower_to_llvm(&mut ctx, module_op_ptr)?;
436	
437	    // Detect CUDA libdevice usage.
438	    //
439	    // Lowering the rustc float-math intrinsics emits `__nv_*` libdevice
440	    // calls (e.g. `__nv_sinf`, `__nv_pow`). `llc` cannot resolve those — they
441	    // need libNVVM + nvJitLink + `libdevice.10.bc`, which the example owns
442	    // (see `examples/device_ffi_test/tools/`). When we see them we:
443	    //   1. Force NVVM IR mode so the `.ll` is suitable for libNVVM input.
444	    //   2. Skip the `llc → .ptx` step, because the resulting PTX would have
445	    //      unresolved `__nv_*` extern calls and `cuModuleLoad` would reject
446	    //      it.
447	    // The example is then expected to feed the `.ll` through the LTOIR
448	    // pipeline (compile_ltoir + link_ltoir) and load the resulting cubin.
449	    let needs_libdevice = module_uses_libdevice(&ctx, module_op_ptr);
450	    let emit_nvvm_ir = config.emit_nvvm_ir || needs_libdevice;
451	    if needs_libdevice && !config.emit_nvvm_ir && config.verbose {
452	        eprintln!(
453	            "\n=== Detected CUDA libdevice (`__nv_*`) calls; \
454	             auto-emitting NVVM IR (skip llc) ==="
455	        );
456	    }
457	
458	    // NVVM IR export must validate its explicit target against the same
459	    // module feature floor as ordinary PTX generation. An ordinary zero-flag
460	    // build can also discover only now that libdevice makes NVVM IR necessary.
461	    // Render one in-memory preview before choosing the final pointer dialect;
462	    // the preview is only inspected and is not written as an artifact.
463	    let automatic_features = if emit_nvvm_ir {
464	        let preview = render_llvm_ir(
465	            &ctx,
466	            module_op_ptr,
467	            device_externs,
468	            false,
469	            None,
470	            config.debug_kind,
471	        )?;
472	        Some(detect_features_in_llvm_text(&preview))
473	    } else {
474	        None
475	    };
476	
477	    // Pre-Blackwell and Blackwell GPUs use different NVVM IR pointer syntax.
478	    // Resolve one concrete target before export and record it with the
479	    // artifact.
480	    let (nvvm_target, nvvm_dialect) = if emit_nvvm_ir {
481	        let target = resolve_nvvm_target(
482	            config.target_arch.as_deref(),
483	            config.device_arch_hint.as_deref(),
484	            automatic_features,
485	        )?;
486	        let dialect = if target.uses_legacy_llvm() {
487	            NvvmIrDialect::LegacyLlvm7
488	        } else {
489	            NvvmIrDialect::Modern
490	        };
491	        validate_nvvm_debug_support(&target, dialect, config.debug_kind)?;
492	        (Some(target), Some(dialect))
493	    } else {
494	        (None, None)
495	    };
496	
497	    // Step 5.5: Convert LLVM operations to the forms supported by the selected
498	    // NVVM dialect, then verify the changed module before text export.
499	    if let Some(dialect) = nvvm_dialect {
500	        if config.verbose {
501	            if dialect == NvvmIrDialect::LegacyLlvm7 {
502	                eprintln!("\n=== Legalizing LLVM dialect for legacy NVVM ===");
503	            } else {
504	                eprintln!("\n=== Legalizing NVVM bit-intrinsic widths ===");
505	            }
506	        }
507	        nvvm_transforms::legalize_for_nvvm(&mut ctx, module_op_ptr, dialect)
508	            .map_err(|error| PipelineError::Lowering(error.disp(&ctx).to_string()))?;
509	    }
510	
511	    // Step 6: Verify the final LLVM dialect module. Dump BEFORE verify so
512	    // verification failures still surface the exact post-legalization IR.
513	    if config.show_llvm_dialect {
514	        eprintln!("\n=== LLVM dialect (pre-verify) ===");
515	        eprintln!("{}", module_op_ptr.deref(&ctx).disp(&ctx));
516	    }
517	    if config.verbose {
518	        eprintln!("=== Verifying LLVM dialect module ===");
519	    }
520	    verify_operation(&ctx, module_op_ptr, "llvm module")?;
521	    if config.verbose {
522	        eprintln!("LLVM dialect verification successful ✓");
523	    }
524	
525	    // Step 7: Export to LLVM IR
526	    if config.verbose {
527	        let mode = if emit_nvvm_ir { "NVVM IR" } else { "PTX" };
528	        eprintln!("\n=== Exporting to LLVM IR ({} mode) ===", mode);
529	    }
530	    let ll_path = config.output_dir.join(format!("{}.ll", config.output_name));
531	    // Remove artifacts from earlier builds so changing output mode cannot
532	    // leave older PTX, LTOIR, or cubin selected by the loader.
533	    clear_stale_compilation_artifacts(&config.output_dir, &config.output_name)?;
534	    let _llvm_ir = export_llvm_ir(
535	        &ctx,
536	        module_op_ptr,
537	        device_externs,
538	        &ll_path,
539	        emit_nvvm_ir,
540	        nvvm_dialect,
541	        config.debug_kind,
542	    )?;
543	    if config.verbose {
544	        eprintln!("LLVM IR written to {}", ll_path.display());
545	    }
546	
547	    // Step 8: Generate PTX or stop at NVVM IR for libNVVM-owned paths.
548	    if emit_nvvm_ir {
549	        // Skip llc. Return a would-be ptx_path so callers see a stable shape;
550	        // the file does not exist and the consumer must build its own cubin
551	        // from `ll_path` via libNVVM + nvJitLink.
552	        let ptx_path = config
553	            .output_dir
554	            .join(format!("{}.ptx", config.output_name));
555	        if config.verbose {
556	            let reason = if needs_libdevice {
557	                "libdevice present"
558	            } else {
559	                "NVVM IR requested"
560	            };
561	            eprintln!("\n=== Skipping llc ({reason}); consumer owns libNVVM/nvJitLink build ===");
562	        }
563	        let target = nvvm_target
564	            .as_ref()
565	            .expect("NVVM target was resolved before export")
566	            .sm();
567	        write_nvvm_target_sidecar(&config.output_dir, &config.output_name, &target)?;
568	        Ok(CompilationResult {
569	            artifact_path: ll_path.clone(),
570	            artifact_kind: CompilationArtifactKind::NvvmIr,
571	            ll_path,
572	            ptx_path,
573	            target,
574	        })
575	    } else {
576	        if config.verbose {
577	            eprintln!("\n=== Generating PTX ===");
578	        }
579	        let ptx_path = config
580	            .output_dir
581	            .join(format!("{}.ptx", config.output_name));
582	        let target = generate_ptx(&ll_path, &ptx_path, config.debug_kind)?;
583	        if config.verbose {
584	            eprintln!(
585	                "✓ PTX written to {} (target: {})",
586	                ptx_path.display(),
587	                target
588	            );
589	        }
590	
591	        Ok(CompilationResult {
592	            artifact_path: ptx_path.clone(),
593	            artifact_kind: CompilationArtifactKind::Ptx,
594	            ll_path,
595	            ptx_path,
596	            target,
597	        })
598	    }
599	}

601	/// Ensures the configured output directory exists before any emission step.
602	///
603	/// The pipeline writes every generated artifact under `PipelineConfig::output_dir`.
604	/// Creating the directory at the pipeline boundary lets callers provide fresh
605	/// sidecar paths without separately seeding them first.
606	fn prepare_output_dir(output_dir: &Path) -> Result<(), PipelineError> {
607	    std::fs::create_dir_all(output_dir).map_err(|e| {
608	        PipelineError::Export(format!(
609	            "failed to create output directory {}: {}",
610	            output_dir.display(),
611	            e
612	        ))
613	    })
614	}

616	/// Returns true when lowering emitted CUDA libdevice calls.
617	///
618	/// Float math intrinsics (sin, cos, exp, log, pow, …) lower to `__nv_*`
619	/// entry points from `libdevice.10.bc`. `llc` cannot resolve these; they
620	/// need libNVVM + nvJitLink + libdevice. When we see any `__nv_*` symbol
621	/// the example owns the LTOIR build (see `examples/device_ffi_test/tools/`).
622	fn module_uses_libdevice(ctx: &Context, module_op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>) -> bool {
623	    op_uses_libdevice(ctx, module_op_ptr)
624	}
625	
626	/// Recursively scan for declared or called CUDA libdevice functions.
627	fn op_uses_libdevice(ctx: &Context, op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>) -> bool {
628	    if let Some(func) = Operation::get_op::<llvm_export::ops::FuncOp>(op_ptr, ctx)
629	        && func.get_symbol_name(ctx).starts_with("__nv_")
630	    {
631	        return true;
632	    }
633	
634	    if let Some(call) = Operation::get_op::<llvm_export::ops::CallOp>(op_ptr, ctx)
635	        && let CallOpCallable::Direct(callee) = call.callee(ctx)
636	        && callee.to_string().starts_with("__nv_")
637	    {
638	        return true;
639	    }
640	
641	    let op_ref = op_ptr.deref(ctx);
642	    for region in op_ref.regions() {
643	        let region_ref = region.deref(ctx);
644	        for block in region_ref.iter(ctx) {
645	            let block_ref = block.deref(ctx);
646	            for child_op in block_ref.iter(ctx) {
647	                if op_uses_libdevice(ctx, child_op) {
648	                    return true;
649	                }
650	            }
651	        }
652	    }
653	
654	    false
655	}

657	/// Recursively verifies an operation and all nested operations.
658	///
659	/// On failure, attempts to find the innermost failing operation for better
660	/// error messages.
661	fn verify_operation(
662	    ctx: &Context,
663	    op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>,
664	    name: &str,
665	) -> Result<(), PipelineError> {
666	    if let Err(e) = op_ptr.deref(ctx).verify(ctx) {
667	        // Try to find specific failing operation
668	        if let Some((err_op, err_msg)) = find_inner_verification_error(ctx, op_ptr) {
669	            return Err(PipelineError::Verification {
670	                name: name.to_string(),
671	                message: err_msg,
672	                operation: Some(err_op.deref(ctx).disp(ctx).to_string()),
673	            });
674	        }
675	
676	        // Use .disp(ctx) to get full error with location and backtrace
677	        return Err(PipelineError::Verification {
678	            name: name.to_string(),
679	            message: e.disp(ctx).to_string(),
680	            operation: None,
681	        });
682	    }
683	    Ok(())
684	}

686	/// Inserts a function operation into the module's block.
687	fn append_to_module(ctx: &Context, module_op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>, func_op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>) {
688	    let region = module_op_ptr.deref(ctx).get_region(0).deref(ctx);
689	    let block = region.iter(ctx).next().expect("Module should have a block");
690	    func_op_ptr.insert_at_back(block, ctx);
691	}

693	/// Lowers `dialect-mir` operations to the LLVM dialect.
694	///
695	/// Runs `mir-lower`'s `DialectConversion`-based pass, which converts each
696	/// `dialect-mir`/`dialect-nvvm` op to its LLVM dialect equivalent. The LLVM
697	/// dialect auto-registers when the `Context` is created, so no explicit
698	/// registration is needed here.
699	fn lower_to_llvm(ctx: &mut Context, module_op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>) -> Result<(), PipelineError> {
700	    mir_lower::register(ctx);
701	
702	    match mir_lower::lower_mir_to_llvm(ctx, module_op_ptr) {
703	        Ok(()) => Ok(()),
704	        // Format with `ctx` so the failing op's location/span survives.
705	        Err(e) => Err(PipelineError::Lowering(e.disp(ctx).to_string())),
706	    }
707	}

709	/// Adds device extern function declarations to the LLVM dialect module.
710	///
711	/// Creates LLVM dialect `FuncOp` declarations (without bodies) for each
712	/// device extern function. These declarations ensure that calls to extern
713	/// functions pass verification; the matching `declare` statements with
714	/// attributes are emitted during LLVM IR export.
715	///
716	/// This runs before MIR-to-LLVM call lowering so the call converter can read
717	/// exact parameter address spaces. It is still idempotent with respect to any
718	/// LLVM declaration already present in the mixed module; inserting a second
719	/// `FuncOp` for the same symbol would fail module verification.
720	fn add_device_extern_declarations(
721	    ctx: &mut Context,
722	    module_op_ptr: Ptr<Operation>,
723	    device_externs: &[DeviceExternDecl],
724	) -> Result<(), PipelineError> {
725	    use llvm_export::ops::FuncOp;
726	    use llvm_export::types::FuncType;
727	    use pliron::builtin::type_interfaces::FunctionTypeInterface;
728	    use pliron::identifier::Identifier;
729	    use std::collections::HashMap;
730	
731	    // Get the module's block pointer first (this is a Ptr, not a Ref, so no borrow issues)
732	    let block = {
733	        let region = module_op_ptr.deref(ctx).get_region(0).deref(ctx);
734	        region.iter(ctx).next().expect("Module should have a block")
735	    };
736	
737	    let declared_symbols: HashMap<_, _> = block
738	        .deref(ctx)
739	        .iter(ctx)
740	        .filter_map(|op| {
741	            Operation::get_op::<FuncOp>(op, ctx)
742	                .map(|f| (f.get_symbol_name(ctx).to_string(), f.get_type(ctx)))
743	        })
744	        .collect();
745	
746	    for decl in device_externs {
747	        let param_types: Vec<_> = decl
748	            .param_types
749	            .iter()
750	            .map(|ty| device_extern_type_to_pliron(ctx, ty, false))
751	            .collect::<Result<_, _>>()?;
752	        let return_type = device_extern_type_to_pliron(ctx, &decl.return_type, true)?;
753	
754	        // Create function type (result, args, is_variadic)
755	        let func_type = FuncType::get(ctx, return_type, param_types, false);
756	
757	        if let Some(existing_type) = declared_symbols.get(&decl.export_name) {
758	            let existing_ref = existing_type.deref(ctx);
759	            let existing = &*existing_ref;
760	            let expected_ref = func_type.deref(ctx);
761	            let expected = &*expected_ref;
762	            if existing.result_type() != expected.result_type()
763	                || existing.arg_types() != expected.arg_types()
764	                || existing.is_var_arg() != expected.is_var_arg()
765	            {
766	                return Err(PipelineError::Export(format!(
767	                    "device extern `@{}` conflicts with the call-site declaration: expected `{}`, found `{}`",
768	                    decl.export_name,
769	                    expected_ref.disp(ctx),
770	                    existing_ref.disp(ctx),
771	                )));
772	            }
773	            continue;
774	        }
775	
776	        // Use the original export name (NOT the prefixed name).
777	        // The MIR sees calls to `cuda_oxide_device_extern_<hash>_foo`, but
778	        // mir-lower/convert/ops/call.rs strips the reserved prefix via
779	        // `reserved_oxide_symbols::device_extern_base_name`, so the LLVM IR
780	        // emits `call @foo(...)`. For that to resolve, we declare `@foo` here.
781	        let func_ident: Identifier = decl.export_name.clone().try_into().map_err(|_| {
782	            PipelineError::Export(format!(
783	                "device-extern symbol `{}` cannot be represented by the LLVM dialect",
784	                decl.export_name
785	            ))
786	        })?;
787	
788	        // Create function declaration (no body = declaration)
789	        let func_op = FuncOp::new(ctx, func_ident, func_type);
790	
791	        // Insert at the front of the module (declarations come before definitions)
792	        func_op.get_operation().insert_at_front(block, ctx);
793	    }
794	
795	    Ok(())
796	}
